manifest.name: Ops
manifest.description: Makemind Ops — per-project operations console for tasks, processes, knowledge, members, skills, profiles, philosophies, and bundles. vibe_studio built-in app: each Ops tab binds a single project directory (standard newProjectInActive / openProjectInActive wiring); members, tasks, skills, processes, philosophies, profiles, and knowledge live inside the project. The seed ships only the shared `ops.manager` agent and operations-manual knowledge that every project reuses.

sources.title: Ops — identity, project layout, surfaces
sources.description: Makemind Ops identity, project structure, sidebar, agent / lifecycle wiring. Entry point for both external LLM clients and the host chat manager.

documents.title: Ops overview — purpose + positioning
documents.content:
# Ops overview

Makemind Ops is a per-project operations console (vibe_studio built-in app `makemind_ops`) — a company-like operating environment where agents and people run work across workspaces.

## Identity
- One Ops tab = one project directory (standard host wiring, like App Builder / Scene Builder). First entry forces New / Open.
- Operations data (members · tasks · processes · skills · profiles · philosophies · knowledge) live inside the bound project. Seed = shared `ops.manager` agent + this operations manual; no operations data.

## Sidebar (14 routes, grouped)
- Overview: Home · Activity (observability)
- Experts: Experts (members)
- Knowledge: Knowledge · Skills · Profiles · Philosophies
- Work: **Inbox** (human approval + task queue) · Tasks · Processes · Workspaces
- System: Bundles · Audit · About
(Chat and Settings are host chrome — `chromeBridge.activeChatAgentId` + the domain panel in the host Settings dialog.)

## Operating model — the 3-axis matrix
Workspace (unit work-space, free granularity + formal sharing) × Org (hierarchy, approval + escalation) × Process (cross-workspace work unit; agents and people; gates, human steps, auto-chaining). People are approvers + doers via the Inbox. See the ops_concepts `matrix_model` doc.

## 5 facades + 9 host capabilities
- Knowledge facades × 5 — Facts · Skills · Profiles · Philosophies · Knowledge files.
- Host capability packs (shared `<id>.*`) — io · channel · browser · fs · db · canvas · kv · analysis · form · ingest (catalog: `studio://knowledge/studio_host_tools/capabilities`).

Runs in hybrid mode on the vibe_studio backbone (`KernelApp.system`); ensureBoot rebinds on project change.

documents.title: Ops project layout — one bound project per Ops tab
documents.content:
# Project layout

Ops uses standard vibe_studio built-in wiring — one Ops tab = one project.

## Forced entry
- Empty tab = wizard (`New project` / `Open project` two buttons).
- User selection → `chromeBridge.newProjectInActive` / `openProjectInActive` → `_bindProject` → KnowledgeInit boot.
- The seed contains only the policy layer (shared `ops.manager` + operations manual). No data (organizations · projects · members · tasks · etc.).

## Project folder layout
```
<currentProject>/                — Ops project root (selected by user via New / Open)
  project.opsproj               — marker (checked by `isOpsProjectDir`)
  _system/                      — reserved (home workspace for `ops.manager`)
  members/                      — agent · person (member.yaml)
  philosophies/                 — editorial principles etc. (yaml)
  processes/                    — multi-step workflow definitions + execution history
  profiles/                     — agent persona definitions (yaml)
  skills/                       — MCP tool definitions + action bodies (yaml)
  tasks/                        — single-shot task queue
  knowledge/                    — Fact · Pattern · Summary · files
  auth/                         — external channel credentials (Keychain · encrypted)
```

## Shared vs per-project
- **Shared (seed)** — `ops.manager` agent + operations manual (`studio://knowledge/ops_*`).
- **Per-project** — assets in the folders above. All operations data added by the user.

## Project switching
- Project Header `Open` or wizard `Open project` — select a different directory.
- `_bindProject` re-invoked → KnowledgeInit rebinds to the new root.

documents.title: Ops lifecycle wiring — chrome bridge contract
documents.content:
# Lifecycle wiring

The Ops shell follows only the standard host chrome wiring (same contract as App Builder / Scene Builder).

## chrome bridge slots
- `chromeBridge.newProjectInActive` — `_newProject({name, parent})` — creates directory + `applyOpsProjectSeed` + `_bindProject`.
- `chromeBridge.openProjectInActive` — `_openProject(path)` — validates with `isOpsProjectDir`, then `_bindProject`.
- `chromeBridge.closeProjectInActive` — `_closeProject()` — `_currentProject = null` + `resetBootCache`.
- `chromeBridge.lifecycleState` — pushes `DomainLifecycleState(hasProject, projectName)` ("No project open" on Home, `basename(dir)` when bound).

## Active-tab gate
IndexedStack eagerly mounts all tab bodies, so the chrome bridge is a process-wide singleton. If two Ops tabs are mounted simultaneously, the later one can capture the slot — all chrome bridge writes must pass the `_isActiveTab` check (`BuiltInAppRegistry.instance.activeContext?.bundlePath == widget.bundlePath`) before executing.

## Boot cache
`OpsBuiltInApp.ensureBoot({backbone, currentProject})` — process-wide cache.
- When `currentProject` changes, a new boot is triggered and the previous boot's facade is disposed.
- Same project + same backbone = cached instance returned.
- `resetBootCache` (closeProject path) registers dispose → the next ensureBoot creates a fresh KnowledgeInit.

documents.title: Ops agents — shared `ops.manager` (seed) + per-project members
documents.content:
# Agents

## `ops.manager` (shared, seed)
- ID: `ops.manager` — registered in the seed's `manifest.agents[]`, shared across all Ops projects.
- Display: "Ops Manager"
- Role: `manager` — vibe_studio's chat routing convention routes chat entry via `role: manager`.
- Tool allowlist = empty list (wildcard). Can call all tools exposed on the host endpoint.
- Host chat = automatically routed to `ops.manager` via `chromeBridge.activeChatAgentId = 'ops.manager'` when the Ops tab is active.
- Auxiliary home workspace = the bound project's `_system/` directory (ensured at boot).

## Project members (per project)
- Defined in `members/*.yaml` within the bound project.
- type = `agent` (LLM) or `person` (external user).
- Agent members have their own LLM model, skill allowlist, and persona (profile).
- Separate from the seed's `ops.manager` — project members participate only in that project's operations flow (publish · review · ads · etc.).

## Delegation calls
- Single-turn member agent call = `agent_ask({agentId:'<member_id>', message})`.
- Batch work for a member agent = `task_create({title, skill, args, assigneeId:'<member_id>'})` or `process_start`.

sources.title: Ops delegation patterns — manager → member / skill / process / task
sources.description: Decision tree for how the manager (ops.manager) delegates or executes when it receives an operations request.

documents.title: Decision tree — user request → call flow
documents.content:
# Delegation decision tree

## 1. Data retrieval / status check
- Single fact → `knowledge_fact_query`. File read/list → `knowledge_file_read` / `knowledge_file_list`.
- List members / skills / processes / bundles → `member_list` / `skill_list` / `process_list` / `bundle_installed`. Config → `config_get`.

## 2. One-shot execution
- Run a skill once = `task_create({skill:'<id>', args, title})` → `task_run`. Result = `task_get({id})`.
- Run a real command / file / db op = a host capability (`io.execute`, `fs.*`, `db.*`) — directly or inside a process step.

## 3. Multi-step workflow
- Defined process exists = `process_start({id})`. Else define with `process_save({yaml})` then start.
- Progress = `process_get({id})` / `process_runs({id})`.

## 4. Human in the loop (matrix)
- A gate awaiting approval → `approvals_pending({approverId?})`; approve = `process_approve({id, approverId})` (designated approver or org-ancestor).
- A human work-step → `tasks_pending({assigneeId?})`; the person completes via `step_submit`.
- Each affects only its own run — everything else keeps running.

## 5. Direct member call
- One-shot to a member = `agent_ask({agentId, message})`. History = `agent_get_history`. Routing = `agent_route({intent})`.

## Priority order
**process > task > direct tool/capability call > agent_ask**. A process provides approval gates, human steps, auto-chaining, and audit. `agent_ask` is for reasoning delegation; simple tool/capability calls the manager makes directly.

documents.title: Member creation / attachment flow
documents.content:
# Member flow

## New agent member
```
member_create_agent({
  id: 'editor.junior',
  displayName: 'Junior Editor',
  profileRef: '<profile_id>',
  philosophyRef: '<philosophy_id>',
  skillIds: ['content_review', 'ad_publish'],
  model: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'claude-sonnet-4-6' }
})
```
(Param names are `profileRef` · `philosophyRef` · `skillIds` — not `profile`/`skills`.)

## Person member
`member_add_person({id, displayName, contact})` — a human teammate; can be a process step's approver (`gates.approverId`) or doer (`assigneeId` + `skillId: human`).

## Workspace attach / detach
- Pull from global pool = `member_attach({memberId})` · Remove = `member_detach({memberId})` · Global list = `member_global_list`.

## Channel credentials
`member_capture_auth({memberId, channel:'slack'|'discord'|...})` — stores token in Keychain.

documents.title: Skill definition flow
documents.content:
# Skill flow

## New skill
`skill_save({id, scope:'workspace'|'agent', body:{description, inputSchema, action}})`.

## Auto-generate
`skill_generate({goal:'...natural language goal...'})` — generates skill definition candidates via LLM. User confirms, then `skill_save`.

## 3-level scope
- **template** — provided by seed / bundle.
- **workspace** — project's `skills/*.yaml`. `skill_save({scope:'workspace'})`.
- **agent** — per-member overlay. `skill_save({scope:'agent', agentId})`.

## Invocation
1:1 skill tool — each skill id is used directly as the tool name. `skill_list` shows the currently available ids.

## Deletion
`skill_delete({id, scope})`. Clean up agent overlays first, then delete the workspace definition.

documents.title: Process (workflow) flow
documents.content:
# Process flow

## Definition (YAML)
`process_save({yaml, workspaceId?})` — the process is a YAML string:
```yaml
id: weekly_publish
title: Weekly Publish
steps:
  - stepId: draft
    assigneeId: editor.junior     # an agent member; OR a person + skillId: human (waits for step_submit)
    skillId: content_review
    inputs: {}
    channelThreadId: deploy        # optional handoff — posts to this in-app thread after the step
gates:
  - approverId: ops.manager        # suspends the run until this approver (or an org-ancestor) approves
triggerSource: upstream_proc_id    # optional — auto-start when that process completes (event chaining)
```

## Execution
`process_start({id, inputs?})` → `{runId, state, currentStep}`. `state: waitingApproval` = paused at a gate; `waitingTask` = paused at a human step.
- Approve a gate = `process_approve({id, approverId})` — must be the designated approver or an org-ancestor (escalation).
- Resume / cancel = `process_resume` / `process_cancel`. History = `process_runs({id})` · single run = `process_get({id})`.

## Patterns (matrix model)
- Cross-workspace: a step's `assigneeId` may be an agent in another workspace — the process spans workspaces.
- Human approval = a `gate` (designated `approverId`; suspends only this run; escalates up the org tree). Human work = a `human` step (queued in `tasks_pending`, completed via `step_submit`).
- Auto-chaining = `triggerSource` (A completes → B starts unattended). Recurring = schedule/cron.

sources.title: Ops 5 facades — knowledge / skill / profile / philosophy / fact
sources.description: The meaning and storage layout of each of Ops's 5 knowledge facades. The basis for semantic distinction when ops.manager composes delegation / retrieval flows.

documents.title: Knowledge facade — fact graph + file store
documents.content:
# Knowledge facade

Free-form knowledge for a project. Two kinds:

## 1. Fact (graph triple)
- Based on mcp_fact_graph. `knowledge_fact_save({subject, predicate, object, source})` · `knowledge_fact_query({subject?, predicate?, object?})`.
- Cross-workspace reads honor formal `workspace_share` grants (a shared scope is merged read-only, tagged with its origin).

## 2. File
- Files under the project's `knowledge/` folder. `knowledge_file_write` / `knowledge_file_read` / `knowledge_file_list({prefix})` / `knowledge_file_delete`.
- `knowledge_ingest_file({path})` — ingest an external file (chunking via the host `ingest.*` capability) into facts.
(No `knowledge_query` tool — retrieval is fact-query + file-list.)

documents.title: Skill facade — MCP tools + action bodies
documents.content:
# Skill facade

MCP tools that the project or its members can invoke. YAML definition + action body (script · prompt · tool chain).

- `skill_list` / `skill_get` / `skill_save` / `skill_delete` / `skill_generate` / `skill_global_list`.
- Scope = template / workspace / agent (see skill_lifecycle in ops_delegation).
- Invocation = skill id is the tool name as-is. Use `skill_list` output to confirm available ids.

documents.title: Profile facade — agent persona
documents.content:
# Profile facade

Persona definition for agent members (voice tone · values · operational context).

- Location = project `profiles/<id>.yaml`.
- Assigned via `profile: '<id>'` when calling `member_create_agent`.
- Multiple agents can share the same profile.
- Merged as part of the LLM system prompt.

documents.title: Philosophy facade — editorial / operational principles
documents.content:
# Philosophy facade

Editorial principles / operational values for the project — the axis an agent should follow when making decisions.

- Location = project `philosophies/<id>.yaml`.
- 4-axis structure (axis name · scale · principles · examples).
- A profile can reference a philosophy → becomes the agent's decision basis.
- Host comparison tool = `philosophy_compare` (Ops UI); agent receives it merged into systemPrompt.

documents.title: Unified index of the 5 facades
documents.content:
# Unified index

`KnowledgeInit.boot` attaches all 5 facades (Facts · Skills · Profiles · Philosophies · Knowledge files).
- Per-facade retrieval = that facade's query/list tool (`knowledge_fact_query` · `skill_list` · `member_list` · …).
- A workspace sees its own facets + any scopes granted to it via `workspace_share` (sandbox + explicit grant).
- External LLM = reads the seed manual via `studio://knowledge/ops_*` resources (host fan-out); shared host-capability catalog via `studio://knowledge/studio_host_tools/*`.

documents.title: Operating model — the 3-axis matrix
documents.content:
Ops runs a company-like operating model on three orthogonal axes; a real operation is a point in their matrix.

## 1. Workspace (unit shared work-space)
`workspace_create({type:'org'|'personal'|'project', ...})`. Granularity is free (a team, a whole company, one project). Sharing between workspaces is **formal**: a private sandbox by default, with explicit `workspace_share` grants (read-only scope) — not transitive full access.

## 2. Org (hierarchy — approval + collaboration)
Workspaces nest into an org tree (`workspace_set_parent` / `workspace_tree`, parent/ancestors). A process approval gate names a `approverId`; if the caller isn't the designated approver, an **org-ancestor** can still approve (escalation).

## 3. Process (work / operation unit)
A `process_save` YAML spans steps across workspaces. Steps are agents or **people** (`skillId: human`). Gates suspend a run for human approval; human steps queue in `tasks_pending`. Processes auto-chain via `triggerSource`. Concurrent same-agent requests are serialized through a per-agent queue (no lost turns).

## People
A member can be an agent or a person; a person is an **approver** (`approvals_pending`) and/or a **doer** (`tasks_pending` → `step_submit`). A human action affects only its own run — everything else keeps running.

sources.title: Ops browser primitive recipes (9 ops)
sources.description: Usage patterns for the 9 host browser tools (browser.page_view · browser.web_search · browser.extract · browser.crawl · browser.monitor · browser.submit_form · browser.download · browser.page_audit_role · browser.page_compare_actors).

documents.title: Meaning of the 9 primitives
documents.content:
# 9 browser primitives

| name | purpose |
|---|---|
| `browser.page_view` | Render a URL page + capture DOM (commonly the first step in a skill). |
| `browser.web_search` | Search engine query. |
| `browser.extract` | Page + selector → structured data. |
| `browser.crawl` | Page + depth → URL tree. |
| `browser.monitor` | URL diff watcher. |
| `browser.submit_form` | Form input / submission (auth · CRUD). |
| `browser.download` | File / media download. |
| `browser.page_audit_role` | Compare a page from a role perspective (UX QA). |
| `browser.page_compare_actors` | Diff the same page across two actors (permission / persona QA). |

All run on the host's shared `browser.*` capability (one mcp_browser engine, owned by the host — built-ins use it, never their own).

documents.title: Research + reporting pattern
documents.content:
# Research flow

```
browser.web_search({query})   // collect candidate URLs
  ↓
browser.page_view({url})      // capture page body
  ↓
browser.extract({url, selector})  // structure the data
  ↓
knowledge_save({...}) /
knowledge_fact_save / knowledge_file_write    // persist to facade
  ↓
skill 'research_and_report'   // generate report (recommended inside a process)
```

documents.title: Publish / channel flow
documents.content:
# Publish flow

```
browser.submit_form({url, fields})  // CMS login or post creation
  ↓
browser.download({url, savePath})   // retrieve attachments or assets
  ↓
channel_notify({channelId, payload})  // Slack / Discord notification
```

The skill `ad_publish` wraps this flow. When defined as a multi-step process, a human approval step can be inserted.

sources.title: Ops tool catalog — actual tool surface on the host endpoint
sources.description: The tool surface visible to ops.manager and external LLM clients — tools registered by McpInbound.registerToolsOn plus 1:1 skill tools.

documents.title: config_*
documents.content:
- `config_get` — full OpsConfig JSON (workspacesRoot · activeWorkspace · llm · mcp · ...).
- `config_reload` — re-reads `~/.makemind-ops/config.yaml`.
- `config_set_llm_provider` · `config_set_mcp_outbound` · `config_set_storage` · `config_set_chromium` — partial updates.
- `system_agent_set_model` — swaps `config.systemAgent.model` only (runtime).

documents.title: workspace_*
documents.content:
- `workspace_list` — workspaces in the current project.
- `workspace_create({type:'org'|'personal'|'project', slug, title?, projectRoot?})` · `workspace_update` · `workspace_rename` · `workspace_delete` · `workspace_switch({slug})`.
- org hierarchy (matrix axis 2): `workspace_set_parent({slug, parentId})` · `workspace_tree` — nest workspaces into an org tree (parent / ancestors); a process approval gate can escalate up this tree.
- formal sharing (matrix — sandbox + explicit grant): `workspace_share({fromWorkspaceId, toWorkspaceId, scope})` · `workspace_shares` — grant a read-only knowledge scope from one workspace to another. No grant = no cross-workspace read.

documents.title: member_*
documents.content:
- `member_list` — members of the current workspace.
- `member_global_list` — global pool across all projects.
- `member_get({id})` · `member_create_agent({...})` · `member_add_person({...})` · `member_update` · `member_delete`.
- `member_attach({memberId})` · `member_detach({memberId})`.
- `member_capture_auth({memberId, channel})`.

documents.title: task_* + process_*
documents.content:
- task: `task_list({state?})` · `task_get({id})` · `task_create({title, skill, args, assigneeId?})` · `task_update` · `task_run({id})` · `task_cancel` · `task_delete` · `task_runs`.
- process: `process_list` · `process_get({id})` · `process_save({yaml, workspaceId?})` (YAML definition — shape in ops_delegation) · `process_start({id, inputs?})` · `process_approve({id, approverId})` · `process_resume` · `process_cancel` · `process_delete` · `process_runs({id})`.
- human inbox (matrix — people as approver + doer): `approvals_pending({approverId?})` (gates awaiting a designated approver, incl. org-ancestor escalation) · `tasks_pending({assigneeId?})` (human work-steps awaiting a person) · `step_submit({processId, stepId, result})` (a person completes their step, resuming the run).

documents.title: knowledge_* + skill_* + bundle_*
documents.content:
- knowledge: `knowledge_fact_save` · `knowledge_fact_query` · `knowledge_file_write` · `knowledge_file_read` · `knowledge_file_list` · `knowledge_file_delete` · `knowledge_ingest_file`. (There is no `knowledge_query`/`knowledge_save` tool — use the fact / file tools.)
- skill: `skill_list` · `skill_get` · `skill_global_list` · `skill_save` · `skill_delete` · `skill_generate`. Plus 1:1 tools named after each registered skill id.
- bundle: `bundle_list` · `bundle_installed` · `bundle_install` · `bundle_uninstall`. opspack: `opspack_export` · `opspack_import`.
- reporting: `status_snapshot` · `diagnostic_export` · `html_report_export`. channel: `channel_notify` (routes through the host `channel.*` capability).
- host capability packs (shared `<id>.*`, not Ops-registered — full catalog at `studio://knowledge/studio_host_tools/capabilities`): `io.*` (sandboxed git/dart/flutter/pub exec) · `channel.*` · `browser.*` · `fs.*`/`db.*` (datastore) · `canvas.*` · `kv.*` · `analysis.*` · `form.*` · `ingest.*`. Process steps drive real work (run commands, read/write files, query a db) through these.

documents.title: agent_* + 9 browser primitives
documents.content:
- agent: `agent_ask({agentId, message})` · `agent_assign_skill` · `agent_get_history` · `agent_route({intent})`.
- browser (9, host capability): `browser.page_view` · `browser.page_audit_role` · `browser.web_search` · `browser.extract` · `browser.crawl` · `browser.monitor` · `browser.submit_form` · `browser.download` · `browser.page_compare_actors`.

Additional: ui_* (debug surface), makemind-ops:// resources (external MCP guide / concepts / workflows / tool-catalog / live-state) and prompts (getting_started · drive_workspace · author_skill · evolve_agent · compose_process).

agents.name: Ops Manager
agents.systemPrompt:
You are the manager for Makemind Ops — a vibe_studio built-in app for project operations. The user talks to you whenever the Ops tab is active. You speak the user's language (Korean if they write in Korean).

Identity:
  • One Ops tab = one project directory. All operational data (members / tasks / skills / processes / profiles / philosophies / knowledge) lives inside that project.
  • Seed assets = you (`ops.manager`) + the operations manual (`studio://knowledge/ops_overview/*` · `ops_delegation/*` · `ops_concepts/*` · `ops_browser_recipes/*` · `ops_tool_catalog/*`).
  • Project members (per-project agents) are separate from you — each has its own LLM, skill whitelist, and persona.

When a project is NOT bound:
  • Guide the user to the wizard's `New project` / `Open project`. The Ops wizard routes through the host chrome bridge slot.
  • Do not try to create a directory yourself — the host chrome's standard wiring handles it.

When a project IS bound — routing rules:
  1. Assess state first — the first action is `config_get` plus the needed `member_list` / `skill_list` / `process_list` / `bundle_installed`.
  2. Simple query / change → call the tool directly (you have authority over the entire Ops tool surface).
  3. Multi-step operations → if a defined process exists, prefer `process_start`. Otherwise define it with `process_save`, then `process_start`.
  4. One-off skill execution → `task_create({skill, args})` → `task_run`.
  5. Delegate reasoning / review to a member → `agent_ask({agentId:'<member_id>', message})`. For free-form intent → recommend candidates with `agent_route`.
  6. Web research → 9 host browser primitives (`browser.page_view` · `browser.web_search` · `browser.extract` · ...) → save with `knowledge_save` when needed.
  7. Do not guess identifiers — confirm any unknown `id` with a list tool first.

Cross-builtin:
  • Other tools on the vibe_studio host endpoint (`studio.*` · `app_builder.*` · `scene.*` · `bk.*`) are exposed on the same surface. Call them only when truly needed, and prioritize your own domain (Ops).
  • The host manager (`studio.manager`) is responsible for routing other built-ins. When the user makes a request like "I want to build an app", guide them to the host manager / app_builder.

Writing:
  • Answer in markdown. For tool results, attach a one-line summary the user can act on plus only the raw output that's needed.
  • If ambiguous, ask once and proceed (especially for destructive actions: delete · cancel · uninstall).
